commit.message:
feat: spec-correct type coercion operations (closes #285) (#336)

Implement all ECMAScript abstract operations for type coercion:

- ToPrimitive(input, hint) with OrdinaryToPrimitive for PlainObject
- ToNumber: full object->ToPrimitive path, hex/octal/binary/Infinity parsing
- ToString: object->ToPrimitive(hint:string)
- ToPropertyKey: ToPrimitive then ToString unless Symbol
- ToObject: primitive wrapper objects via PlainObject
- ToInt32/ToUint32/ToInt16: spec-correct modulo truncation
- SameValue: distinguishes -0/+0, NaN == NaN
- SameValueZero: NaN == NaN, -0 == +0
- IsLooselyEqual: full 10-step algorithm with ToPrimitive/ToNumber coercion
- IsStrictlyEqual: no coercion, IEEE 754 semantics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
CRITICAL: Fix render-blocking Mermaid script causing poor Lighthouse scores

PROBLEM IDENTIFIED:
❌ Mermaid.js was loaded synchronously, BLOCKING first paint
   - FCP (First Contentful Paint): 0.01 seconds (needs > 1s)
   - LCP (Largest Contentful Paint): 0.06 seconds (needs > 2s)
   - Speed Index: 0.24 (extremely poor)
   - TTI (Time to Interactive): 8.2 seconds
   - Overall Performance Score: Very low due to render-blocking

ROOT CAUSE:
The Mermaid CDN script was render-blocking. Even though we have diagrams,
they're not critical for page structure/layout. They can load async.

SOLUTION IMPLEMENTED:
✅ Changed Mermaid from SYNC to ASYNC loading:
   - OLD: <script src="..."></script><script>mermaid.contentLoaded();</script>
   - NEW: <script async src="..." onload="mermaid.contentLoaded();"></script>

BENEFITS:
- Page now renders WITHOUT waiting for Mermaid
- Non-blocking: Mermaid loads in background after DOM ready
- Still initializes diagrams on load via onload callback
- Browser can paint immediately (FCP improves dramatically)
- Time to Interactive (TTI) reduced from 8.2s to ~1-2s expected

UPDATED:
✓ All 3 existing posts fixed
✓ Template updated for all future posts
✓ Committed to GitHub

EXPECTED LIGHTHOUSE IMPROVEMENT:
- FCP: 0.01s → 1-2s ⬆️ (should score 90+)
- LCP: 0.06s → 2-3s ⬆️ (should score 80+)
- Speed Index: 0.24 → 2-3s ⬆️ (should score 85+)
- Performance Score: 30 → 80+ ⬆️⬆️

Next test: Re-run Lighthouse report to confirm improvements

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Prevent `wasm run` fallthrough to OCI for uninstalled manifest-key components (#240)

* Initial plan

* Prevent run before install: abort with actionable error for uninstalled components

When `wasm run <component>` is invoked with a scope:component-style input
that is not listed in [components] in wasm.toml, the command now aborts
with a user-friendly error and an actionable hint:

- If the component is in the local cache, suggests `wasm run -g <component>`.
- If the component is found in the package index, suggests `wasm run -i <component>`.
- Otherwise, suggests `wasm install <component>`.

Adds spec requirement r[run.not-installed] and integration test.

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: improve cache matching precision and add documentation

- Use exact/suffix matching for cache lookup instead of substring contains
  to avoid false positives (e.g. wasi/http no longer matches wasi/http-proxy)
- Add comments explaining the scope:component → scope/component transformation
- Document WIT naming convention in looks_like_manifest_key
- Document best-effort Manager fallback behavior

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>

* Split r[run.not-installed] spec into separate rules per condition

Split the single spec rule into three:
- r[run.not-installed] — base error for uninstalled manifest keys
- r[run.not-installed.hint-cache] — cache hint with --global/-g
- r[run.not-installed.hint-registry] — registry hint with --install/-i

commit.message:
Add AI Mastering Suite data model and processing framework

Implements MasteringAssistant — an intelligent mastering chain orchestrator
that analyzes audio and applies a complete mastering chain (iZotope Ozone
equivalent).

Header (MasteringAssistant.h):
- AudioAnalysis struct with LUFS, true peak, dynamic range, spectral
  balance, per-band energy, and AI-detected genre/mood
- MasterTarget enum for format presets (Streaming, CD, Vinyl, Broadcast,
  Film, Podcast, Club, Reference, Custom)
- Full mastering chain parameter structs: StemEQ, DynamicEQ,
  MultibandComp, StereoImager, Exciter, Maximizer, TonalBalance
- EBU R128 LoudnessMeter struct
- MasteringAssistant class with pImpl pattern

Implementation (MasteringAssistant.cpp):
- EBU R128 K-weighted loudness measurement with biquad filters
- FFT-based spectral analysis (per-band energy, spectral centroid)
- True peak detection with oversampled interpolation
- Stereo correlation and width analysis
- suggestChain: AI-driven parameter selection based on analysis and target
  format (spectral correction, dynamic range matching, stereo imaging,
  genre-aware presets)
- matchToReference: spectral/loudness/dynamics/width matching
- applyChain: offline processing with dynamic EQ, multiband compression,
  M/S stereo imaging, harmonic exciter, true-peak limiter
- Gated loudness metering with momentary/short-term/integrated readings

commit.message:
feat(apple-silicon): NEON-accelerated attention SV dispatch (#3357)

## Summary

Adds NEON SIMD acceleration to the scores·V (SV) multiplication in the
CPU attention kernel, completing the NEON fast-path for the entire
attention computation on Apple Silicon.

### Changes

- **`neon_sv_into`**: NEON-accelerated SV kernel using `float32x4_t` (4
floats/iter) with scalar tail
- **`dispatch_sv_into` / `dispatch_sv`**: Runtime dispatch selecting
NEON on aarch64, scalar elsewhere
- **Production call sites**: Both `single_head_attention` and
`multi_head_attention` now use dispatched SV
- **Parity test**: `dispatch_sv_matches_scalar` verifies NEON matches
scalar for 9 dim sizes
- **Dead code annotations**: `scalar_sv`/`scalar_sv_into` marked
`#[allow(dead_code)]` (still used by tests)

### Testing

- All 1303 attention tests pass
- Clippy clean (`-D warnings`)
- NEON-vs-scalar parity verified across dim sizes [1, 2, 3, 4, 7, 8, 15,
16, 64]

### Impact

Completes NEON acceleration for both halves of the attention hot loop:
1. Q·K^T dot product (PR #3355)
2. Scores·V multiply (**this PR**)

Together, these eliminate all scalar fallback code from the attention
critical path on Apple Silicon.

**Apple Silicon team** 🍎

commit.message:
Fix confusing error message for unsupported output format; remove plain-text as advertised format (#11220)

# Description

This PR improves the CLI output format handling by:

1. **Fixing the confusing error message** — previously, passing an
unsupported output format produced a vague error. Now it clearly tells
the user which format was rejected and lists the supported formats
(`json`, `table`).
2. **Removing `plain-text` as an advertised output format** —
`plain-text` was only accepted by `rad recipe-pack show` and produced
output identical to `table`. It is no longer shown in help text or flag
descriptions.
3. **Maintaining backwards compatibility** — users who pass `--output
plain-text` (e.g. in scripts) will silently get table-formatted output
instead of an error.

## Changes

### Output format normalization
- Added `NormalizeFormat()` in `pkg/cli/output/formats.go` that maps
`"plain-text"` → `"table"`
- Added `FormatPlainText` case in `NewFormatter()` that returns
`TableFormatter` (defense in depth)

### Unified validation
- Rewrote `RequireOutput()` in `pkg/cli/clivalidation.go` to normalize
`"plain-text"` → `"table"` before validation
- Deleted `RequireOutputAllowPlainText()` and
`requireOutputFromFormats()` — no longer needed
- Deleted `AddOutputFlagWithPlainText()` from
`pkg/cli/cmd/commonflags/flags.go`

### Command updates
- `rad recipe-pack show`: switched from `AddOutputFlagWithPlainText` +
`RequireOutputAllowPlainText` to standard `AddOutputFlag` +
`RequireOutput`
- `rad version`: switched from manual flag reading to
`cli.RequireOutput()` for consistent validation; registered output flag
via `commonflags.AddOutputFlag(cmd)` in `NewCommand()`

### Tests
- Updated `Test_RequireOutput`: `"plain-text"` now expects `"table"`
(not an error)
- Removed `Test_RequireOutputAllowPlainText` (function deleted)
- Added `pkg/cli/output/formats_test.go` — tests for `NormalizeFormat`
- Added `pkg/cli/output/formatter_test.go` — tests for `NewFormatter`
including `"plain-text"` case
- Added table format test case in
`pkg/cli/cmd/recipepack/show/show_test.go`

## Backwards Compatibility

| User input | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `rad app list -o json` | JSON output | JSON output (unchanged) |
| `rad app list -o table` | Table output | Table output (unchanged) |
| `rad app list -o plain-text` | Error: unsupported format | **Table
output** (backwards compat) |
| `rad app list -o text` | Error: unsupported format | Error:
unsupported format (unchanged) |
| `rad app list --help` | "json, table" | "json, table" (unchanged) |
| `rad recipe-pack show X -o plain-text` | Table output (accepted) |
Table output (still accepted, normalized) |
| `rad recipe-pack show X -o table` | Error: unsupported format |
**Table output** (now accepted) |
| `rad recipe-pack show --help` | "plain-text, json" | **"json, table"**
(consistent) |

**Key point**: `"plain-text"` was never meaningfully different from
`"table"` — it always produced the same output. This change makes the

slightly past 60 minutes.

docs: add comprehensive MVP test scenarios (#1)

* docs: add comprehensive MVP test scenarios

Test scenarios covering:
- Authentication & Authorization (Keycloak integration)
- Match Management (CRUD operations)
- Event Management with ownership rules
- Shot image capture for heat maps
- Image storage in Cloud Storage
- Data validation
- Integration tests

Key Requirements:

- Opponents can only add score events
- Score events (2/3 points) require shot location image
- Images stored in Google Cloud Storage for heat map generation
- 36 total test cases across 6 categories

* docs: update requirements with shot coordinates instead of images

Key Changes:
- Score events require shot coordinates (x,y) instead of image storage
- User selects position on visual court image UI component
- Only coordinates stored in database (not images)
- Coordinates used to generate player/team heat maps
- Updated data structure with ScoreEvent coordinates field
- Added business rules for event ownership and match lifecycle

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add missed shot events with coordinates

Key Changes:
- New event type: Missed Shot (arremessos que não geraram pontos)
- Missed shots require coordinates (x,y) like made shots
- Opponents can register missed shots for opponent team (not just scores)
- Heat maps now show both made and missed shots
- Updated data structure with MissedShotEvent type
- Added 15+ test cases for missed shot functionality
- Expanded coordinate storage and heat map generation tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add free throw events with FIBA rules

Key Changes:
- New event type: Free Throw (lances livres)
- Free throws based on foul type (personal, technical, flagrant):
  - Personal foul (no bonus): 1 free throw
  - Personal foul (team bonus - 4+ fouls): 2 free throws
  - Technical foul: 1 free throw
  - Flagrant foul: 2 free throws + ball possession
- Each free throw: 1 point (made) or 0 (missed)
- NO coordinates (fixed line position)
- Tracked separately: made vs missed free throws
- Opponents can register free throws for opponent team
- Updated data structure with FreeThrowEvent and team foul tracking
- Added 13+ test cases for free throw functionality
- Bonus calculation after 4 team fouls per period (FIBA rules)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add period timing and event timestamp tracking

Key Changes:
- Match structure: 4 periods (10 minutes each - FIBA standard)
- Each period tracked: number, startTime, endTime
- Each event includes 3 timestamps:
  - Absolute timestamp (when event occurred)
  - Period number (1-4)
  - Period timestamp (seconds within period, 0-600s)
- Period timestamps enable:
  - Score evolution graphs over time
  - Points per quarter analysis
  - Timeline visualizations
- Updated data structure with Period type
- Event validation: period number (1-4), timestamp (0-600s range)
- Added 9+ test cases for period/timeline functionality

This allows displaying point progression throughout the match and analyzing team/player performance by quarter.
